author: @sailingoctopus1
text:
I'd like to see Luke and Rob build a full-sized wind turbine from scratch, rather than building stuff from scrap. I get the appeal of building stuff from scrap, but it's not something that the audience can go out and reproduce because they are unlikely to be able to source the same parts and, to be honest, the end result is not much use. Whereas, if you build a full-sized wind turbine with a diameter of, say, 2m and you put it on the top of a 20m tower you can, in windy locations, generate a serious amount of power (circa 1kW) that can be used for something useful like charging an electric car or power wall.

I understand that Rob shies away from big projects because of the way YouTube works and audience loss of interest over time, but there's so much to learn from a big project like this; so many new skills to acquire, so much multi-disciplinary knowledge to learn and so much technology to employ.

Luke jokingly says that Rob basically ignores him, which is sad, if true. I'd love to see you working together like master craftsman and apprentice, with Luke asking all the dumb questions that we, the audience, would love to ask.

I like to see Luke build the projects that Rob designs, as that tests the design and gives us, the audience, the confidence to try it out ourselves. After all, if Luke can do it anyone can (no offence intended).

author: @stuartevans6807
text: Nice work, I would love to see a comparison with a 6 blade turbine. Not technically as efficient as the increased wind resistance will mean a slower top speed than the 3 blade version. However the increased torque coupled with a timing belt gear could make up for the difference. You wont get double the watts but should get more than 2 and at lower wind speeds

author: @totherarf
text:
"Rob just ignores me!" ....... As Rob walks strait past the camera! ..... Gold!

One point on measurements Luke (and Rob too) ...... Measuring just the Volts or just the current  and multiplying to get power is not right! Unless the internal resistance is the same you get just Volts and Amps without any relation to each other!
The way to do it is to get a resistance across the generator and measure the Volt Drop across it (or the current running through it ... makes no difference) and apply Ohms Law to work out the other value and the actual Power 

Volts= Current x Resistance .... You can expand it to get Power = Volts x Current! 

Still, another one under your belts! (well you were both in this one) ;o)

text: Because these Motors produce more electrical energy at higher speeds and have low resistance to rotation would not make more sense to use 2 blades instead of 3 as that would generate higher speeds  (admittedly at the cost of torque)

author: @BoB4jjjjs
text:
Always wanted to build a wind turbine with a car alternator just to see what I could generate. 
I have seen small wind turbines generate enough to keep an electric fence going with just a very small battery out of a motorcycle.  It generated enough to create enough high tension voltage to keep sheep where they were supposed to be.
So it more then kept the battery charged.  It was only a small six bladed wind generator like the ones you see on small sailing boats/yachts.

text:
Watching that Volt reading and Amp reading reach 2V and 1A when I was wasn't sure if it would .. WOW very impressive. 
Did you use a Anemometer for wind speed or was it inferred? I've often thought about some 'map' that the met office could produce or that citizen scientists could create that would indicate how common various wind speeds are at different locations - that way we'll know where to put them / what's possible to scavenge !

author: @kreynolds1123
text:
Great job!

I say this with love and respect. Try a few less "so"s.  I think I counted 30 times in this video. 

So 👈😳, try using the word so less as a linguistic setup or introduction so 👈😉 as to prevent over using it. 

I dont want to come across as a grammar nazi (as imperfect and unqualified as I am unknowingly and sometime knowingly  breaking many rules and having terrible spelling and long run on sentences like this one), but maybe considering this little advice on "so" might help make you a little better speaker.  😁

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text: Nice. Keep in mind that the power producer should be measured under load. When you only connect a volt meter no current is flowing and load is zero. When you only connect An amp meter no voltage is over the meter and load on the electrical connections is zero. However in the latter case mechanical load on the generator is present and delivered as Heat in the generator itself. Put a resistor on IT and test. Theres An optimaal resistor value where delivered electrical power maxes out.

text: Usually turbines with Moore blades trurn slower, but I don't se why you couldn't try and give it 6-8 sorter blades, to try and raise the speed of rotation so that you get closer to 5 volts with the same sort of wattage if not a bit more.

replies.author: @tremail564
replies.text: Why the even number blade sets that sounds odd. Because the even blade will be at balance at any point on the turn  where odd find the equilibrium 2 points per blade ,low blade count

replies.author: @William_Hada
replies.text: Yes, and in practice all one has to do is build the generator on a swivel and attach a wind vain tail fin to it. The force of the win on tail fin will always keep it aligned properly to the wind.

replies.author: @marz.6102
replies.text: @William_Hada the problem is if you want it in a big turbine it'll exhaust the energy it made in the first place so maybe multiple small turbines that can easily adjust with little amounts of energy needed.

replies.author: @bugz000
replies.text: some of the giant turbines you see on hills actually face "backward" to what you expect, the blades themselves can create enough drag to pull the turbine round so long as the blades are on the trailing edge - the hardest part is allowing infinite 360 rotation as the cable will always find a way to get coiled up... one of the benefits of VAWT i guess

author: @MikesAllotment
text: Car fan motors typically run off 12v, so logically I would have thought that if you were to manually spin one really fast it would generate significantly more than just 1.7v ?   You produced a really decent current of 0.5 Amps - so I'm kinda surprised the voltage was so low?  Is it normal for a 12v brushless dc motor to only generate <2v ?   Maybe one for Rob....?

replies.author: @tremail564
replies.text: It's about the rpms and for the 12volt fan I think they spin like 1500-2500 rpm.

For example, if a motor produces 100 rpm at 12v 1a input will that mean it will produce 12v and 1a output at 100 rpm?  Or is there a formula that will tell you what to expect?

replies.author: @tremail564
